title: Type 3 Control Channel
established: Separating control from data — evolved into unified message protocol

title: Push-Phase Memoization
established: RESOLVED signal for transitive skip — carried forward

title: Explicit Deps
No-Default-Dedup
established: Operators transparent by default — carried forward

title: Output Slot
Lazy Tier 2
established: get() doesn't guarantee freshness — evolved into status-based trust

title: Promise Elimination
established: Internal APIs return sources not Promises — carried forward

title: Vision: LLM Actuator
established: Three-layer vision, Graph as universal output — directly triggered GraphReFly

title: Unified Spec: Protocol, Single Primitive, Graph Container
topic: Designing the GraphReFly unified cross-repo spec through a 7-step process. Radical simplification from callbag-recharge's 6 primitives + 4 callbag types to 1 primitive (node) + unified message format.
decisions: One primitive: node(deps?, fn?, opts?) — behavior from configuration, sugar constructors for readability
Unified message format: always [[Type, Data?], ...], 9 types, no channel separation
Unified node interface: .get() (cached, never errors), .status, .down(), .up(), .unsubscribe(), .meta
Meta as companion stores — each key subscribable, replaces all with*() wrappers
No separate Knob/Gauge/Inspector — describe() + observe() on Graph
Pure wire edges — no transforms, everything is a node
Colon namespace — "system:payment:validate"

title: Web3 Integration Sketch: Security, OMS, Agent Commerce
topic: Research into how GraphReFly maps onto Web3 security monitoring ($3.4B lost in 2025), AI agent commerce (x402/ERC-8004/ERC-8183), and the missing reactive order management layer for decentralized systems.
decisions: GraphReFly needs no new primitives for Web3 — just adapters (fromChainEvents, fromX402, toTransaction) and domain graph factories
GraphReFly runs off-chain as the coordination brain; contracts stay on-chain
Three detailed graph topology sketches: security monitor, cross-chain order lifecycle, multi-agent job workflow
Custom message types leverage the open message type set (spec §1.2)

title: Message Type Extensibility Analysis
topic: Evaluating whether TS and Python type definitions allow users to define and emit custom message types, as required by the spec's open message type set.
decisions: TypeScript: open — Message = readonly [symbol, unknown?] accepts any symbol
Python: closed — Message = tuple[MessageType, Any] restricts to StrEnum. Needs widening to MessageType | str
Smart contract language comparison (Solidity, Rust, Move, Vyper, Cairo) and why GraphReFly is off-chain

title: Built-in ABAC: Actor, Guard, Policy Builder
topic: Designing built-in access control for GraphReFly that replaces external authz libraries (e.g. CASL). The graph is the single enforcement point.
decisions: Three primitives: Actor context (who), capability guard (may they), scoped introspection (what can they see)
policy() declarative builder — CASL-style allow()/deny() DX, zero dependencies, ~50 LOC
Attribution pulled to Phase 1.5 — node.lastMutation records { actor, timestamp }
CASL rejected as dependency — its subject model, sift.js, pack/unpack unnecessary when graph is only enforcement point
Web3 identity maps cleanly — wallet signatures, x402 proofs, ERC-8004 agent IDs all produce actors
roadmap_impact: New Phase 1.5 (Actor & Guard), expanded Phase 1.6 (tests), Phase 5.4 accepts actor?, Phase 6 simplified

title: Spec Compliance, Patterns, callbag-recharge Parity, Tooling Gaps
topic: Structured 16-batch audit of graphrefly-ts and graphrefly-py against GRAPHREFLY-SPEC.md, internal API consistency, callbag-recharge lessons, operator/data-structure edge cases, and AI-facing introspection.
decisions: Phase A (batches 1-3): strong PASS on core protocol, node, and Graph; open TS vs Py batch-drain deferral inconsistency
Phases B-C (4-8): pattern and pitfall audits; documented fixes
Batch 13: superset-deps pattern correct but may recompute on irrelevant dep changes
Batch 14: RxJS alignment mostly good; naming divergences for LLM ergonomics
Batch 15: describe()/observe() insufficient for causal debugging; proposes structured inspect

title: Reactive Issue Tracker: Dogfooding GraphReFly for Project Management
topic: Designing a reactive issue tracker / agentic knowledge graph that uses graphrefly-ts to solve its own project management pain.
decisions: Issues are live assertions, not status cards — every issue carries a verify() function
Fixed ≠ verified — the graph re-runs verifiers and only transitions to verified on evidence
Regression detection via reactive propagation — file/git changes trigger re-verification
Typed relationships (affects, blockedBy, relatedTo) enable graph queries
AI-native via observe() + annotate() — agents subscribe, get pushed updates

title: Tier 2 Operator Parity: forwardInner, nonlocal, sample Architecture
topic: Cross-repo parity analysis and fixes for three divergences in Python's Tier 2 operators.
decisions: _forward_inner emitted flag — prevents double-DATA when inner emits during subscribe
nonlocal replaces [value] list-boxing — idiomatic Python 3 across all 18 operators
sample rewritten to dep+onMessage — eliminates mirror node
Tier 2 regression testing is the top testing priority

title: Seamless Snapshot/Hydration: Auto-Checkpoint & Node Factory Registry
topic: Designing zero-friction resume for dynamic graphs — auto-checkpoint and node factory registry for fromSnapshot reconstruction.
decisions: Auto-checkpoint fires after settlement — filter to DATA/RESOLVED (phase-2 messages), debounce ~500ms
Incremental snapshots via Graph.diff() — reduces I/O from O(graph_size) to O(changed_nodes)
Factory registry by name glob pattern — not by node type or custom meta field
Guards reconstruct from data — policyFromRules(snap.value.rules) rebuilds guard fns
Topological reconstruction order — mounts → state/producer → derived/operator/effect → edges → restore values

title: Demo & Test Strategy: 4 Demos, GraphReFly-Powered Shell, Reactive Layout, Inspection-as-Test-Harness
topic: Designing the demo and test strategy for Phase 4 domain layers. Four demos each exercising 3+ domain layers with 10-12 numbered acceptance criteria.
decisions: Three-pane shell IS a GraphReFly graph — shell bugs are library bugs
Reactive layout engine (Pretext-on-GraphReFly) — DOM-free text measurement as state → derived pipeline
Acceptance criteria prevent descoping — 10-12 specific, testable ACs per demo
Inspection layer IS the test harness — observe(), describe(), toMermaid() simultaneously showcases, stress-tests, validates
Four-layer test strategy — unit, scenario, inspection stress, adversarial
Eight foreseen building blocks
Non-LLM demos first — Demo 1 and 3 ship before Demo 2 and 4
WebLLM for browser demos — Qwen3 via WebGPU, zero API keys
roadmap_impact: New Phase 7.1-7.6 in TS repo, 7.1-7.4 in PY repo

title: Agentic Memory SOTA Synthesis + Default agentMemory() Strategy
topic: Synthesizing SOTA agentic memory research into a concrete default strategy for agentMemory() (Phase 4.4).
decisions: Four strategies map to existing primitives — 3D filtering funnel, GraphRAG, dynamic reflection, hot/cold tiers
agentMemory() is composition, not a new primitive — wires distill() + knowledgeGraph() + vectorIndex() + collection() + decay() + autoCheckpoint()
Default strategy is opinionated but overridable — OpenViking decay formula
LLM extraction/consolidation stays in userland adapters, never inside core
Five unique advantages over all existing systems

title: Universal Reduction Layer: Massive Info → Actionable Items via LLM-Composable Reactive Graphs
topic: Generalizing GraphReFly from domain-specific tools to a universal reactive reduction layer for any massive info → actionable items pattern.
decisions: GraphReFly is not an observability tool or an issue tracker — it's the reactive computation layer
Three things LLMs unlock: composing the graph, operating inside nodes, auditing decisions
Stratified reduction is the killer differentiator
CICD moment for information processing
10 concrete advantages documented

title: Adoption Blockers: Memory Footprint, DAG-CBOR Codec, Tiered Representation, NodeV0 Promotion
topic: Investigating practical blockers for adoption at scale: runtime memory, serialization overhead, hydration latency.
decisions: DAG-CBOR as default codec — replaces JSON for wire/checkpoint, ~40-50% smaller
Tiered representation — hot (JS objects), warm (DAG-CBOR + lazy hydration), cold (Arrow/Parquet), peek (FlatBuffers)
Five memory tuning strategies — lazy meta, structural sharing, bounded history, struct-of-arrays, dormant eviction
Delta checkpoints more impactful than any format choice — ~400x smaller than full snapshots at steady state
NodeV0 should move from Phase 6 to Phase 3.x — minimum enabler for delta checkpoints, wire-efficient sync

title: Marketing, Promotion & Domain Strategy
topic: Comprehensive marketing strategy for GraphReFly — package descriptions, domain setup, Cloudflare, site architecture, AI discoverability, callbag-recharge deprecation, and phased promotion roadmap.
decisions: Single Starlight site per language — graphrefly.dev (TS) and py.graphrefly.dev (PY)
Three positioning pillars — The Missing Middle, Durable by Default, Inspectable AI Orchestration
AI-permissive crawling — robots.txt allowing GPTBot/ClaudeBot/PerplexityBot + llms.txt
Phased promotion — Foundation → Soft Launch → Growth → Sustained
callbag-recharge deprecation — npm deprecate, README banner, blog migration
Public asset pipeline — robots.txt and llms.txt at repo root, synced by sync-docs.mjs

title: First-Principles Audit: Is GraphReFly Necessary?
topic: Deep self-examination of whether GraphReFly is over-engineered, starting from first principles.
decisions: GraphReFly is not over-engineered — optimized for human+LLM reactive co-operation bet
Irreducible core is five things: node, edge, propagation, describe/snapshot, GraphSpec
Graph > functions for LLM composition — constrains error space like SQL vs hand-written DB ops
Causal chain persistence is the killer argument — not reactivity, not performance
No LoRA/SFT needed if GraphSpec stays simple enough for zero-shot
Demo 0 (The Existential Demo) designed: NL → GraphSpec → simplified flow → run → persist → causal explain
Three-layer DX/UX strategy: LLM-DX, Dev-DX, End-user UX — Layer 3 is the real moat
Reposition: lead with user pain points, not architecture
research_findings: Callbag (2018) had near-identical thesis, died from zero ecosystem
Industry converged on Signals for sync, Observables for async
Virtual threads killed server-side reactive in Java; coordination-protocol positioning remains viable
HN feedback: sounds good but don't understand the solution — confirms architecture-first messaging fails

title: Competitive Analysis: DeerFlow 2.0 & Deep Agents vs GraphReFly
topic: Research-backed comparison of DeerFlow 2.0 (ByteDance, 32k stars) and LangChain Deep Agents (19.3k stars) against GraphReFly.
decisions: DeerFlow's 1.0→2.0 evolution trajectory points at GraphReFly — steps 4-7 already provided
DeerFlow is a harness, GraphReFly is a protocol — different abstraction layers, composable
File system as state is 2026 consensus but has fundamental limits
Tool-as-Agent vs Topology-as-Program is the core philosophical divide
Deep Agents validates the pattern without depth
Complementary is market entry tactic, not architectural truth
Ecosystem infiltration via 3 entry points: MCP Server, workspace bridge, LangGraph adapter

title: Harness Engineering Gap Analysis, Three-Wave Announcement Plan, Eval System Design, Ecosystem Infiltration
topic: Research-backed gap analysis between harness engineering (2026's defining trend) and GraphReFly's existing architecture.
decisions: Anti-stealth: moat is category ownership, not code secrecy. Window April-June 2026
Three announcement waves: Wave 1 (eval), Wave 2 (harness layer + MCP + scorecard), Wave 3 (Demo 0 + Show HN)
Two-tier eval: portable/manual + automated/API. LLMProvider interface for model switching
MCP Server as primary infiltration vector — one npm package reaches all MCP clients
explainPath is THE differentiator — causal walkback, P0 priority
Reposition from reactive graph engine to reactive harness layer for agent workflows
roadmap_impact: New §9.1-9.7 in TS roadmap, §9.0/9.2/9.2b in PY roadmap, §15 in marketing strategy

title: Reactive Collaboration Harness: Static-Topology Loop for Human+LLM Co-operation
topic: Designing a 7-stage static-topology loop (intake → triage → queue → gate → execute → verify → reflect) for human+LLM co-operation.
decisions: Static topology + flowing data — graph models the workflow (pipes), not work items (water)
gate.modify() IS structured human judgment — ported from callbag-recharge with Array.map-style signature
Cursor reading as dimensionality reduction — converts exponential graph branching into linear sequence
promptNode factory — universal LLM transform: any prompt + deps → processing node
Strategy model (intervention effectiveness) — derived node tracking rootCause×intervention → successRate
distill() kept for memory, not replaced by promptNode
Existing infrastructure covers ~70% — new work: gate port (~200 LOC), promptNode (~100 LOC), valve rename, wiring (~430 LOC)
Boolean gate → valve rename — frees gate for the real human-approval primitive
Build as §9.0 before 9.1b — dogfood the loop while running the 4-treatment eval experiment

title: Inspection Tool Consolidation: 14+ Tools → 9, TS + PY Parity
topic: Reducing inspection tool surface from 14+ exports to 9 non-overlapping tools across TS and PY. Triggered by debugging PY gate.modify() test exposing tool sprawl and invariant violations.
decisions: spy() merges into observe(format=) — spy is observe+logger, no independent logic
annotate()+traceLog() merge into trace() — one method, write+read overloads
4 RxJS bridge functions consolidate to single toObservable(source, opts?)
describeNode/metaSnapshot unexported — internal plumbing for describe()
Polling _wait_for replaced by filter|take(1) reactive composition — no new primitive needed
harnessTrace() added — wires observe() to all 7 harness stages for pipeline visibility
Graph.diff() ported to PY — snapshot comparison
Runner __repr__ added — pending task counter for diagnostic dumps
Pre-1.0: breaking changes, no aliases or legacy shims
invariants_enforced: §5.9 No imperative triggers — harnessTrace surfaces reactive flow gaps
§5.10 No raw Promises — Runner repr shows missing runner
§5.12 Dev-friendly — 9 tools vs 14+ reduces cognitive load
pending: PY gate.modify() test needs re-validation with new inspection tools

title: Mid-Level Harness Blocks: graphLens, resilientPipeline, guardedExecution, persistentState
topic: Designing composed building blocks between raw primitives and harnessLoop() — the missing mid-level layer for power users building custom harness variants.
decisions: Three-layer API stack: primitives → mid-level blocks → harnessLoop()
graphLens() — observability as reactive nodes (topology, health, flow, why), never natural language
resilientPipeline() — encodes correct nesting order (rateLimiter→breaker→retry→timeout→fallback)
guardedExecution() — ABAC + policy + budgetGate as composable safety wrapper
persistentState() — autoCheckpoint + snapshot + restore + incremental diff bundled
Four blocks not eight — 8 requirements cluster naturally, some already standalone (gate, agentMemory)
Library never generates natural language — structured data only, LLMs/UIs render
roadmap_impact: New §9.0b in TS roadmap, between §9.0 (collaboration loop) and Wave 1 (eval story)
